volumeTitle: Casina. The Casket Comedy. Curculio. Epidicus. The Two Menaechmuses
label: The Two Menaechmuses (Section 3.2.487b - Section 4.2.571)

sections.label: Section 3.2.487b
sections.original: Quid ais , homo
sections.translation: What say you , you fellow lighter than a feather , most rascally and most abandoned — you disgraceful man — you cheat , and most worthless fellow ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.490
sections.original: quid de te merui , qua me causa perderes ?
sections.translation: Why have I deserved this of you ? For what reason should you ruin me ? How you stole yourself away from me just now at the Forum . You ’ ve been performing the funeral of the breakfast in my absence . Why did you dare to do so , when I was entitled to it in an equal degree ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.494
sections.original: Adulescens , quaeso , quid tibi mecum est rei ,
sections.translation: Young man , prithee , what business with me have you ,

sections.label: Section 3.2.495
sections.original: qui mihi male dicas homini ignoto insciens ?
sections.translation: who are thus purposely insulting a person whom you know not ? Do you wish a punishment to be given you for your abuse ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.497
sections.original: † Pol eam quidem edepol te dedisse intellego .
sections.translation: Do be quiet ; by my faith , I discover that you ’ ve done that already indeed .

sections.label: Section 3.2.498
sections.original: Responde , adulescens , quaeso , quid nomen tibist ?
sections.translation: Answer me , young man , I beg ; what is your name ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.499
sections.original: Etiam derides , quasi nomen non noveris ?
sections.translation: Are you laughing at me , as well , as though you didn ’ t know my name ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.500
sections.original: Non edepol ego te , quod sciam , umquam ante hunc diem
sections.translation: By my troth , I never saw or knew you , that I ’ m aware of , before this day ; but at all events , whoever you are , if you do what ’ s right , you won ’ t be an annoyance to me .

sections.label: Section 3.2.503
sections.original: Menaechme , vigila .
sections.translation: Menaechmus , awake .

sections.label: Section 3.2.503b
sections.original: Vigilo hercle equidem , quod sciam .
sections.translation: I ’ troth , I really am awake , so far as I know .

sections.label: Section 3.2.504
sections.original: Non me novisti ?
sections.translation: Don ’ t you know me ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.504b
sections.original: Non negem , si noverim .
sections.translation: I shouldn ’ t deny it if I did know you .

sections.label: Section 3.2.505
sections.original: Tuom parasitum non novisti ?
sections.translation: Don ’ t you know your own Parasite ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.505b
sections.original: Non tibi
sections.translation: Young man , I find that your headpiece isn ’ t sound .

sections.label: Section 3.2.507
sections.original: Responde , surrupuistin uxori tuae
sections.translation: Answer me ; have you not purloined that mantle from your wife to-day , and given it to Erotium ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.509
sections.original: Neque hercle ego uxorem habeo neque ego Erotio
sections.translation: I ’ faith I have no wife , nor have I given the mantle to Erotium , nor have I purloined it .

sections.label: Section 3.2.510b
sections.original: Satin sanus es ?
sections.translation: Are you really in your senses ? ⋯ This matter ’ s settled [*] . Did I not see you coming out of doors clad in a mantle ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.512b
sections.original: Vae capiti tuo .
sections.translation: Woe to your head . Do you think that all people are effeminate rogues [*] because you are one ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.514_515
sections.original: tun med indutum fuisse pallam praedicas ?
sections.translation: Do you declare that I was clothed in a mantle ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.516
sections.original: Ego hercle vero .
sections.translation: Troth , I really do .

sections.label: Section 3.2.516b
sections.original: Non tu abis quo dignus es ?
sections.translation: Why don ’ t you go where you are deserving to go , or else request yourself to be atoned for , you downright madman ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.518
sections.original: Numquam edepol quisquam me exorabit , quin tuae
sections.translation: By my troth , never shall any one prevail upon me not to tell your wife the whole matter now , just as it happened .

sections.label: Section 3.2.520
sections.original: omnes in te istaec recident contumeliae :
sections.translation: All these insults shall be retorted upon yourself . I ’ ll take care that you shan ’ t have devoured the breakfast unpunished .

sections.label: Section 3.2.522
sections.original: Quid hoc ést negoti ? satine , ut quemque conspicor ,
sections.translation: What ’ s the meaning of this business ? Why , Just as I see each person , do they all make fun of me in this way ? But the door makes a noise .

sections.label: Section 3.3.524
sections.original: Menaechme , amare ait te multum Erotium ,
sections.translation: Menaechmus , Erotium says that she entreats you much ,

sections.label: Section 3.3.525
sections.original: ut hoc una opera sibi ad aurificem deferas ,
sections.translation: that at the same opportunity you ’ ll take this to the goldsmith ’ s , and add to it an ounce in weight of gold , and order the bracelet [*] to be fashioned anew . ( Gives him a bracelet .)

sections.label: Section 3.3.528
sections.original: Et istúc et aliud , si quid curari volet ,
sections.translation: Tell her that I ’ ll attend both to this and anything else that she shall wish , if she wishes anything else attended to .

sections.label: Section 3.3.530
sections.original: Scin quid hoc sit spinter ?
sections.translation: Do you know what this bracelet is ?

sections.label: Section 3.3.530b
sections.original: Nescio , nisi aureum .
sections.translation: I don ’ t know , unless it ’ s of gold .

sections.label: Section 3.3.531
sections.original: Hoc est quod olim clanculum ex armario
sections.translation: This is the same one that you once said that you had secretly stolen out of the closet from your wife .

sections.label: Section 3.3.533
sections.original: Numquam hercle factum est .
sections.translation: By my troth , ’ twas never done .

sections.label: Section 3.3.533b
sections.original: Non meministi , obsecro ?
sections.translation: Prithee , don ’ t you remember it ?

sections.label: Section 3.3.534
sections.original: redde igitur spinter , si non meministi .
sections.translation: Give it me back then , if you don ’ t remember it . ( Tries to take it .)

sections.label: Section 3.3.534b
sections.original: Mane .
sections.translation: Stop . ( Pretends to examine the bracelet .)

sections.label: Section 3.3.535
sections.original: immo equidem memini . nempe hoc est , quod illí dedi .
sections.translation: O yes , I really do remember it ; it ’ s the same , I believe , that I presented to her .

sections.label: Section 3.3.536
sections.original: Istuc .
sections.translation: I ’ faith , it is the same .

sections.label: Section 3.3.536b
sections.original: Vbi illae armillaé sunt , quas una dedi ?
sections.translation: Where are the clasps which I gave her together with them ?

sections.label: Section 3.3.537
sections.original: Numquam dedisti .
sections.translation: You never gave her any .

sections.label: Section 3.3.537b
sections.original: Nam pol hoc unum dedi .
sections.translation: Why , faith , I gave them together with this ⋯

sections.label: Section 3.3.538
sections.original: Dicam curare ?
sections.translation: Shall I say that you ’ ll attend to it ?

sections.label: Section 3.3.538b
sections.original: Dicito : curabitur .
sections.translation: Do say so ; it shall be attended to .

sections.label: Section 3.3.539_540
sections.original: et palla et spinter faxo referantur simul .
sections.translation: I ’ ll take care that the mantle and the bracelet are brought back together .

sections.label: Section 3.3.541
sections.original: Amabo , mi Menaechme , inauris da mihi
sections.translation: My dear Menaechmus , do , pray , give me some earrings [*] , the pendants to be made two didrachms in weight ; that I may look on you with delight when you come to our house .

sections.label: Section 3.3.544
sections.original: Fiat . cedo aurum , ego manupretium dabo .
sections.translation: Be it so . Give me the gold [*] ; I ’ ll find the price of the workmanship .

sections.label: Section 3.3.545
sections.original: Da sodes abs te : ego post reddidero tibi .
sections.translation: Give it yourself , please ; at a future time I ’ ll give it you back .

sections.label: Section 3.3.546
sections.original: Immo cedo abs te : ego post tibi reddam duplex .
sections.translation: No , give it yourself ; at a future time I ’ ll give it you twofold .

sections.label: Section 3.3.547
sections.original: Non habeo .
sections.translation: I haven ’ t any .

sections.label: Section 3.3.547b
sections.original: At tu , quando habebis , tum dato .
sections.translation: But when you have it , do you give it me , then .

sections.label: Section 3.3.548
sections.original: Numquid me vis ?—
sections.translation: Do you wish for aught ?

sections.label: Section 3.3.548b
sections.original: Haec me curaturum dicito —
sections.translation: Say that I ’ ll attend to these things , ( aside ) to be sold as soon as they can , and for what they ’ ll fetch .

sections.label: Section 3.3.550
sections.original: iamne abiit intro ? abiit , operuit fores .
sections.translation: Has she now gone off in-doors ? She ’ s gone , and has shut the door . Surely all the Gods are favouring , amplifying , and prospering me . But why do I delay while opportunity and time are granted me to get away from these procurers ’ dens ? Make haste , Menaechmus ; pull foot and quicken your pace .

sections.label: Section 3.3.555
sections.original: demam hanc coronam atque abiciam ad laevam manum ,
sections.translation: I ’ ll take off this chaplet [*] , and throw it away on the left hand side ( throws the chaplet down ) , that , if they follow me , they may think I ’ ve gone in that direction . I ’ ll go and meet my servant , if I can , that he may learn from me these blessings which the Gods confer upon me .

sections.label: Section 4.1.559
sections.original: Egone hic me patiar frustra in matrimonio ,
sections.translation: And shall I allow myself to remain in wedlock [*] here ,

sections.label: Section 4.1.560
sections.original: ubi vir compilet clanculum quidquid domist
sections.translation: when my husband secretly pilfers whatever ’ s in the house , and carries it thence off to his mistress ?

sections.label: Section 4.1.561b
sections.original: Quin tu taces ?
sections.translation: Why don ’ t you hold your peace ? I ’ ll let you now catch him in the fact ; do you only follow me this way . ( They go to the opposite side of the stage .) In a state of drunkenness , with a chaplet on , he was carrying the mantle to the embroiderer ’ s , which he purloined from you at home to-day .

sections.label: Section 4.1.565
sections.original: sed eccám coronam quám habuit . num mentior ?
sections.translation: But see , here is the chaplet which he had on . ( Seeing the chaplet on the ground .) Now am I saying false ? Aha , this way has he gone , if you wish to trace his footsteps . And , by my faith , see , here he comes on his way back most opportunely , but he isn ’ t wearing the mantle .

sections.label: Section 4.1.568b
sections.original: Quid ego nunc cum illoc agam ?
sections.translation: What now shall I do to him ?

sections.label: Section 4.1.569
sections.original: Idem quod semper : male habeas ; sic censeo .
sections.translation: The same as usual ; abuse him .

sections.label: Section 4.1.570
sections.original: huc concedamus : ex insidiis aucupa .
sections.translation: Let ’ s step aside this way watch him from ambush . ( They retire on one side .)

sections.label: Section 4.2.571
sections.original: Vt hóc utimúr maximé more móro
sections.translation: How we do practise a custom here that is very foolish and extremely troublesome , and how even those who are the most worthy and great [*] do follow this habit : all wish their dependants to be many in number ;
